DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC,, LEM WONG'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. (PART 1 OF 2 RULING) PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE A SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS ALONG WITH THE MOVING PAPERS, AS REQUIRED BY THE CCP. PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS DO NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH THE EXACT NATURE OF THE RELIEF REQUESTED IN THE MOTION TO COMPEL. PLAINTIFF FILED THE SEPARATE STATEMENT ALONG WITH THE REPLY BRIEF, BUT THIS WAS OF NO HELP TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT WAS, THEREFORE, UNABLE TO ADEQUATELY OPPOSE THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION. THIS ALONE COULD SERVE AS THE BASIS FOR DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO DOES NOT APPEAR TO HAVE ADEQUATELY MET AND CONFERRED WITH DEFENDANT BEFORE FILING THIS MOTION, WHICH ALSO MAY HAVE ALLOWED DEFENDANT TO BETTER UNDERSTAND THE MOTION AND THE GROUNDS FOR OPPOSITION. IN LIGHT OF THE REGULAR AND FREQUENT CONTACT BETWEEN THE PARTIES, PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE DONE MORE TO ALERT DEFENDANT TO ITS INTENTION TO FILE THE MOTION TO COMPEL TO ALLOW FOR INFORMAL RESOLUTION BETWEEN THE PARTIES AND TO ALLOW DEFENDANT TO BETTER UNDERSTAND THE DISPUTE. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT THE MOTION IS TIME-BARRED IS NOT WELL TAKEN IN LIGHT OF DEFENDANT'S INABILITY TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE RESPONSES WERE VERIFIED UNTIL MAY 5, 2014. (END OF PART 1 OF 2 RULING)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC,, LEM WONG'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS.(CONTINUED PART 2 OF 2 RULING)THIS, ALONG WITH THE ROLLING NATURE OF THE PRODUCTION, COULD HAVE REASONABLY LED PLAINTIFF TO BELIEVE THAT DEFENDANT HAD NOT FULLY COMPLETED ITS RESPONSES UNTIL THAT DATE. EVEN AFTER THE COURT REVIEWED THE SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS, IT IS NOT CLEAR WHAT RELIEF PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING. IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT HAS PRODUCED ALL INFORMATION RESPONSIVE TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS AND HAS SUBMITTED A VERIFIED RESPONSE. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DID NOT ADEQUATELY MEET AND CONFER WITH DEFENDANT, DID NOT FOLLOW THE CCP'S REQUIREMENTS WHEN FILING THE MOTION, AND THE MOVING PAPERS DO NOT PROVIDE SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION. PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ARE THEREFORE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY SANCTIONED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000 TOTAL TO ALLOW DEFENDANT CAN RECOVER COSTS IN OPPOSING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION (CODE OF CIV. PROC. 2031.310(H). (END OF 2 OF 2 OF RULING) DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND EMAIL JUDGE PRO TEM AN ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE PRO TEM: CONOR MOORE, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CLARE MACY, CSR#5256, (415-981-3498), REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT HAROLD WONG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT SERVE, OR CAUSE TO BE SERVED, A NOTICE TO CONSUMER. (CODE CIV. PROC., 1985.3.) THE STATEMENT IN PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL'S DECLARATION THAT "UPON INFORMATION AND BELIEF" AMERICAN EXPRESS/DATAMARK'S AGENT INFORMED COUNSEL THAT A "NOTICE" HAD BEEN PROVIDED TO DEFENDANT REGARDING THE SUBPOENA IS INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY. (STAR MOTORS IMPORTS, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1979) 88 CAL.APP.3D 201, 204.) THE DECLARATION ALSO DOES NOT SPECIFY WHAT "NOTICE" WAS ALLEGEDLY PROVIDED, I.E. WHETHER IT WAS THE NOTICE REQUIRED BY SECTION 1985.3. THE STATUTE'S LANGUAGE IS MANDATORY. (SEE 1985.3, SUBD. (B) [PROVIDING THAT THE SUBPOENAING PARTY "SHALL" GIVE NOTICE TO THE CONSUMER OR CAUSE NOTICE TO BE GIVEN].) THEREFORE, COMPLIANCE WITH THE NOTICE PROVISION IS REQUIRED BEFORE A CONSUMER'S RECORDS CAN BE PRODUCED. SINCE THE NOTICE WAS NOT SERVED HERE, THE SUBPOENA MUST BE QUASHED. THE TIMELINESS OF THE MOTION IS NOT AN ISSUE; THE COURT MAY ENTERTAIN A MOTION TO QUASH EVEN AFTER THE DATE SET FOR PRODUCTION. (SLAGLE V. SUPERIOR COURT (1989), 211 CAL.APP.3D 1309, 1312.) Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jamie Mak, Esq. of The Meridian Law (415-306-0838) for Lem Sek Wong, et al. Plaintiff and moving party through Court Call
Patrick O' Brian, Esq of Daniel Crowley & Associates (707-525-8999 ext 106) for Harold Wong Defendant and opposing party through Court Call
Court Reporter: Clare Macy, CSR#5256, (415-981-3498)

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFs L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC,, LEM WONG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents. Plaintiff failed to file a separate statement of facts along with the moving papers, as required by the CCP. Plaintiff's moving papers do not otherwise provide Defendant with the exact nature of the relief requested in the motion to compel. Plaintiff filed the separate statement along with the reply brief, but this was of no help to Defendant. Defendant was, therefore, unable to adequately oppose the Plaintiff's motion. This alone could serve as the basis for denying Plaintiff's motion. Plaintiff also does not appear to have adequately met and conferred with Defendant before filing this motion, which also may have allowed Defendant to better understand the motion and the grounds for opposition. In light of the regular and frequent contact between the parties, Plaintiff should have done more to alert Defendant to its intention to file the motion to compel to allow for informal resolution between the parties and to allow Defendant to better understand the dispute. Defendant's argument that the motion is time-barred is not well taken in light of Defendant's inability to demonstrate that the responses were verified until May 5, 2014. This, along with the rolling nature of the production, could have reasonably led Plaintiff to believe that Defendant had not fully completed its responses until that date. Even after the Court reviewed the separate statement of facts, it is not clear what relief Plaintiff is seeking. It appears that Defendant has produced all information responsive to Plaintiff's requests and has submitted a verified response. Accordingly, Plaintiff's motion to compel is denied and Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendant is denied. Plaintiff did not adequately meet and confer with Defendant, did not follow the CCP's requirements when filing the motion, and the moving papers do not provide substantial justification for Plaintiff's motion. Plaintiff and Plaintiff's counsel are therefore jointly and severally sanctioned in the amount of $1,000 total to allow Defendant can recover costs in opposing Plaintiff's motion (Code of Civ. Proc. 2031.310(h). Defendant's Counsel to prepare and email Judge Pro Tem an order approved as to form. Judge Pro Tem: Conor Moore, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Clare Macy, CSR#5256, (415-981-3498), Reported. =(302/JPT)

Ntc Of And Mtn To Quash Service Of Subpoena Duces Tecum
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No Appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HAROLD WONG's Motion To Quash Service Of Subpoena Duces Tecum is granted. Plaintiffs did not serve, or cause to be served, a notice to consumer. (Code Civ. Proc., 1985.3.) The statement in Plaintiffs' counsel's declaration that "upon information and belief" American Express/Datamark's agent informed counsel that a "notice" had been provided to Defendant regarding the subpoena is inadmissible hearsay. (Star Motors Imports, Inc. v. Superior Court (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 201, 204.) The declaration also does not specify what "notice" was allegedly provided, i.e. whether it was the notice required by section 1985.3. The statute's language is mandatory. (See 1985.3, subd. (b) [providing that the subpoenaing party "shall" give notice to the consumer or cause notice to be given].) Therefore, compliance with the notice provision is required before a consumer's records can be produced. Since the notice was not served here, the subpoena must be quashed. The timeliness of the motion is not an issue; the Court may entertain a motion to quash even after the date set for production. (Slagle v. Superior Court (1989), 211 Cal.App.3d 1309, 1312.) Judge Pro Tem to prepare an order. Judge Pro Tem: James Weixel, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
